title: pinus flexilis subsp. albicaulis
intro: Pinus flexilis subsp. albicaulis, also known as limber pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the western United States and southwestern Canada, characterized by its twisted branches and reddish-brown bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling, heal wounds, and combat infections. The medicinal properties of Pinus flexilis subsp. albicaulis have been attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds like alpha-pinene, beta-pinene, and limonene, offering health benefits such as relief from respiratory issues, improved immune function, and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of limber pine include infused oils, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds, or taken internally to promote overall well-being.
